claimText: the vaccine for newborns made those tragedies preventable.
postText: A doctor in Alaska once watched the hepatis B virus take children’s lives, until the vaccine for newborns made those tragedies preventable. Now a CDC panel is weighing changes that could limit access to the shot and fuel misinformation. @JackieFortier

claimText: Americans' confidence in the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has plummeted.
postText: Americans' confidence in the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has plummeted, according to a 2025 National Foundation for Infectious Diseases survey, and many young people are getting their advice on vaccines from social media.

claimText: Misinformation about Hepatitis B and the shot has grown rampant in recent years.
postText: "Misinformation about Hepatitis B and the shot has grown rampant in recent years, largely thanks to anti-vaccine activists such as Kennedy," the Editorial Board writes.

claimText: A years-long campaign to falsely link vaccines and autism has evolved from online conspiracy to federal guidance.
postText: A years-long campaign to falsely link vaccines and autism has evolved from online conspiracy to federal guidance — reshaping the CDC, weakening disease surveillance, and endangering public health.

claimText: the CDC’s new claim that vaccines cannot be ruled out as a cause of autism will lead to years of confusion and vaccine refusal.
postText: The CDC’s new claim that vaccines cannot be ruled out as a cause of autism will lead to years of confusion and vaccine refusal, @Northeastern public health experts say.

claimText: Harmful myths suggesting that vaccines cause autism sow fear and distrust in vaccines.
postText:
Fact-checked
Harmful myths suggesting that vaccines cause autism sow fear and distrust in vaccines, ultimately jeopardizing the great progress we’ve made in preventing serious childhood diseases. Read our full fact-check to learn more:

claimText: vaccines cannot be definitively ruled out as an autism cause.
postText: Public health experts are expressing significant concerns about widespread confusion and potential misinformation stemming from a new CDC claim suggesting that vaccines cannot be definitively ruled out as an autism cause.
